title: My AI Art Workflow - How I Create Cinematic Anime Visuals With Stable Diffusion
excerpt: A practical look at prompt planning, reference gathering, iteration, upscaling, and final art direction.
content:
Start with direction

I begin with the mood, camera language, palette, and story beat before touching a prompt. A clear visual target makes every generation easier to judge.

Build the prompt in layers

The first layer defines subject and scene. The second adds lens, lighting, and material detail. The third removes noise with negative prompts and quality constraints.

Iterate like a designer

I compare batches, save strong seeds, upscale only the best candidates, and finish with small composition adjustments. The final image should feel intentional, not accidental.

Why it helps my dev work

Creative direction makes interfaces stronger. It trains the same eye for hierarchy, mood, and detail that polished products need.
